findings.evidence: `rg -n "transpose|relabelRows|relabelColumns|swapRows|swapColumns" TauCeti/KnotTheory/Grid/Diagram.lean` shows relabel/swap APIs at lines 171-329 and transpose only at lines 432-457, with no transpose/relabel or transpose/swap theorem.
findings.fix: Add characteristic lemmas such as `(x.relabelRows ρ).transpose = x.transpose.relabelColumns ρ` and `(x.relabelColumns κ).transpose = x.transpose.relabelRows κ`, plus swap specializations where useful, with `@[simp]` if oriented to a normal form.
findings.issue: `GridState.transpose` has no public interaction lemmas with `relabelRows`/`relabelColumns` or their `swapRows`/`swapColumns` specializations, even though these are the adjacent public symmetry operations.

findings.evidence: The diagram symmetry API exposes component, membership, involution, and composition lemmas for relabel/swap at lines 570-752, while the transpose block at lines 758-806 only gives components, involution, set images, and membership.
findings.fix: Add diagram-level lemmas mirroring the state API, e.g. `(G.relabelRows ρ).transpose = G.transpose.relabelColumns ρ` and `(G.relabelColumns κ).transpose = G.transpose.relabelRows κ`, plus swap versions if the swap API is intended as first-class.
findings.issue: `GridDiagram.transpose` likewise lacks row/column exchange lemmas for diagram relabelings and swaps, so users cannot compose diagram symmetries without unfolding component definitions.

summary: The transpose operation exposes the basic point-set and grading invariance API, but it is not characterized against the row/column symmetries already public in the same lane. Downstream symmetry arguments still have to unfold `transpose`, `relabelRows`, and `relabelColumns` to compose these operations.
